description: Hamburg port strike: 3-week backlog. Air freight premium +40%.
US 25% aluminium tariff. +7% spot price. Margin squeeze on US-aluminium-exposed vendors.
China rare earth quota cut. Neodymium +28%. 6-week lead time crisis.
EU anti-dumping duties 18.7% on Chinese electrical components. Guangzhou loses price advantage.
JPY weakness causes Japanese vendors to raise export prices 6-9%.
Suez rerouting: Asia-Europe +12-14 days. Indian and Chinese vendor buffer stock depleted.
Cerro Negro Q2 weak results; CFO signals aggressive pricing H2 2024.
Tanaka capacity expansion rumour — if true, lead times drop 30% H2.
Hexagon AG capacity ramp 30%: competing for same vendor pool. Supply tightness Q1 2025.
Helios Micro Systems onboarded. Analogs: Vantage Sensors + Bright Spark.
Andean Precision SRL first RFQ. Analogs: Adriatic Fastenings + Türk Makina.
Sahara Industrial FZE evaluated. Analogs: Cerro Negro + Coastal Alloys.
Meridian cash flow stress peaks Sep-Nov 2024; payment lateness increases; Kovacs and Fischer flag concerns.
Patel Alloys win rate climbs to 38% after 9 months of forecast sharing; Meridian shortlists them preferentially.
Guangzhou Mech & Elec effectively exits Meridian vendor pool; health score 31; no responses to last 4 RFQs.
